---
title: "LRP74: Does word reading route through nonword decoding? (floor-limited mediation)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The revised DAG labels nonword reading $N$ the **"code route (mediator)"** — the decoding
step between the phonics skills and word reading $W$. Yet every existing mediation model
routes reading through the phonics *knowledge* directly, skipping the decoding *behaviour*.
LRP74 fills that gap: a single-mediator g-formula decomposition of the intervention's
word-reading effect through $N$. Because nonwords cannot be sight-read, a route through $N$
isolates genuine decoding in a way a route to $W$ does not.

## Design

Phase 0 only, $n \approx 50$. Mediator $N_{t2}$ (Beta-Binomial on $N_{t1}$); outcome
$W_{t2}$. Adjustment $\{G, A, E, R, W_\text{pre}, N_{t1}\}$ — vocabulary $E, R$ are the
mediator–outcome confounders (the whole-word route that bypasses decoding). Letter sounds
$L$ and blending $B$ are *upstream* of $N$, so they are on the $G \to \dots \to N$ path and
are **not** adjusted.

## Assumptions — read this first {#sec-assumptions}

Two caveats specific to this model, beyond the usual ID-2 ($GA$-confounding, treatment-induced
dose confounder):

- **$N$ is heavily floored** in this cohort (the documented $NW$ floor; a corrected DS
  decoding deficit $g \approx -0.89$). If the intervention barely moves $N$ off the floor the
  indirect effect is $\approx 0$ with wide intervals — read that as **floor-limited**, not as
  evidence of no route.
- **Direction is contested in DS.** Roch & Jarrold (2012) found the *longitudinal* arrow
  running $WR \to NW$ (and level-gated), so the forward $NW \to WR$ mediation imported from TD
  is only weakly corroborated in DS. This is an adjusted association under a **stated,
  DS-uncertain** forward direction.

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
